revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:源泉]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11564196]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|qq=|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|qq=|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|qq=|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|qq=|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|qq=|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|qq=|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

<gallery widths="210 px" heights="160 px">
File:Beppu Umi-jigoku04n4272.jpg|{{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|qq=}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|qq=|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|qq=|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|qq=|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|qq=|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|qq=|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|qq=|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|qq=|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|qq=|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:

These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}

[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen yunohana huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:

***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:源泉]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11564196]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

<gallery widths="210 px" heights="160 px">
File:Beppu Umi-jigoku04n4272.jpg|{{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Chinoike-jigoku03n3200.jpg|{{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Shiraike-jigoku02n4272.jpg|{{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Tatsumaki-jigoku03s5s4272.jpg|{{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|qq=}}
</gallery>

* '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property:'''
** {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|別府明礬温泉の湯の花製造技術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯の花|1=Sinter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563426}}
<gallery widths="210 px" heights="160px">
File:Myoban Onsen 02.jpg|{{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
</gallery>

* '''Other Attractions:'''
** {{ill|Noboribetsu Onsen|ja|登別温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noboribetsu Onsen|1=Noboribetsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3888689}}'s "Jigokudani"
** {{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}}'s "Ōbuki"
** [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s "Yubatake"
** {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}}'s "Yubatake"
** {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}'s "Source Facility"
** [[:en:Hakone Onsen|Hakone Onsen]]'s "Ōwakudani"
** {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}'s "Ogama"
** {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}'s "Arayu"
** [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]]'s "Yudutsu"
** [[:en:Unzen Onsen|Unzen Onsen]]'s "Unzen Jigoku"

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}

* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}

* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11564196]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. === Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11564196]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}

<gallery widths="210 px" heights="160 px">
File:Beppu Umi-jigoku04n4272.jpg|{{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Chinoike-jigoku03n3200.jpg|{{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Shiraike-jigoku02n4272.jpg|{{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|qq=}}
File:Beppu Tatsumaki-jigoku03s5s4272.jpg|{{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|qq=}}
</gallery>

* '''Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property:'''
** {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|別府明礬温泉の湯の花製造技術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯の花|1=Sinter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563426}}
<gallery widths="210 px" heights="160px">
File:Myoban Onsen 02.jpg|{{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
</gallery>

* '''Other Attractions:'''
** {{ill|Noboribetsu Onsen|ja|登別温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noboribetsu Onsen|1=Noboribetsu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3888689}}'s "Jigokudani"
** {{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}}'s "Ōbuki"
** [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s "Yubatake"
** {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}}'s "Yubatake"
** {{ill|Ikaho Onsen|ja|伊香保温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Onsen|qq=}}'s "Source Facility"
** [[:en:Hakone Onsen|Hakone Onsen]]'s "Ōwakudani"
** {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}'s "Ogama"
** {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}'s "Arayu"
** [[:en:Yunomine Onsen|Yunomine Onsen]]'s "Yudutsu"
** [[:en:Unzen Onsen|Unzen Onsen]]'s "Unzen Jigoku"

=== Facilities Directly Over Source Springs ===
In most cases, water from source springs is piped or channeled to bathing facilities. However, some naturally discharging springs are located directly under bathing facilities (called ashi-moto-yushutsu). This is only possible if the spring's temperature is close to optimal for bathing. Such springs are relatively rare due to narrow temperature ranges. Examples include:

* '''Hokkaido:''' {{ill|Niseko Yakushi Onsen|ja|ニセコ薬師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niseko Yakushi Onsen|1=Niseko Yakushi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324369}}
* '''Aomori:''' {{ill|Sukayu Onsen|ja|酸ヶ湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukayu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酸ヶ湯|1=Sukayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7635737}}
* '''Akita:''' [[:en:Nyūtō Onsen|Nyūtō Onsenkyō]], including {{ill|Tsurunoyu Onsen|ja|鶴の湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurunoyu Onsen|qq=}}
* '''Gunma:''' {{ill|Shiriyaki Onsen|ja|尻焼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiriyaki Onsen|1=Shiriyaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464890}}, {{ill|Hōshi Onsen|ja|法師温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshi Onsen|1=Hōshi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555123}}
* '''Toyama:''' {{ill|Kanezuri Onsen|ja|鐘釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanezuri Onsen|1=Kanetsuri Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651337}}
* '''Wakayama:''' {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|ja|川湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|1=Kawayu Onsen (Wakayama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478934}}
* '''Okayama:''' {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}
* '''Ōita:''' {{ill|Myoban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}'s mud baths
* '''Kumamoto:''' {{ill|Jigoku Onsen|ja|地獄温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigoku Onsen|1=Jigoku Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424486}}

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Hotspring Tamagawa Akita 01.jpg|thumb|right|240 px|Naturally discharging Spring Mouth ({{ill|Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|ja|玉川温泉|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring|lt=Tamagawa Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Onsen (Akita Prefecture)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Hot Spring|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572582}} Ōbuki).]]
[[File:Arima Onsen Tenjin Sengen.JPG|thumb|right|180px|Drilled self-discharging Spring Mouth ([[:en:Arima Onsen|Arima Onsen]] Tenjin Source).]]

'''Spring Mouth''' (源泉, gensen) refers to a location where water emerges from underground. Beyond its literal meaning, the term is used metaphorically to describe the origin of something, such as ideas or monetary sources (e.g., "源泉徴収" or "withholding tax" and "アイデアの源泉" or "the source of ideas").

== Spring Mouths in Onsen ==
In the context of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], a Spring Mouth is the location where thermal spring water emerges from underground. There are three main types of spring discharge forms:
* '''Natural discharge:''' Springs that emerge naturally from cracks in the rock or the ground.
* '''Drilled self-discharge:''' Springs that emerge due to pressure after drilling a borehole and installing a pipe.
* '''Drilled powered extraction:''' Springs pumped from deep underground using mechanical power after drilling.

Natural discharge source springs can emerge due to natural phenomena like earthquakes or volcanic eruptions. Drilled springs involve artificial development, often essential for establishing new onsen facilities. In [[:en:Beppu|Beppu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], drilling techniques like the {{ill|Kazusa-bori method|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa-bori method|qq=}} were introduced in 1879 (Meiji 12), leading to the active development of source springs for various uses, including {{ill|onsen ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} baths, public communal baths, and private residential use. This transformed [[:en:Beppu Onsen|Beppu Onsen]] into a renowned {{ill|spa town|ja|温泉都市|en|lt=onsen city|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spa town|1=spa city|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562970}}.<ref>Beppu has the most source springs (2,847) and the highest daily discharge volume (137,040 kiloliters/day) in Japan (as of 2000, Ministry of the Environment).</ref>

=== Source Springs as Tourist Attractions ===
While many source springs, particularly drilled types, are not open to the public and consist of simple pipes or concrete structures, some have become tourist destinations. These include {{ill|National Places of Scenic Beauty|ja|名勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Places of Scenic Beauty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752}} like the four springs of {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}} ("The Hells of Beppu") and traditional facilities like the {{ill|Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja|明礬温泉湯の花小屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoban Onsen Sinter huts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or [[:en:Kusatsu Onsen|Kusatsu Onsen]]'s {{ill|Yubatake|ja|湯畑|1=Yubatake|2=ja|3=湯畑|4=zh|5=汤畑|6=de|7=Onsen-Felder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubatake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563768}}.

* '''National Places of Scenic Beauty:'''
** Springs in {{ill|Kannawa Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannawa Onsen|1=Kannawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30921762}} and {{ill|Shibaseki Onsen|ja|柴石温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibaseki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4890932}}, collectively known as {{ill|Beppu no Jigoku|ja|別府の地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu no Jigoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府地獄めぐり|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}, including:
***  {{ill|Umi Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#海地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umi Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Chinoike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#血の池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Shiraike Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#白池地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
***  {{ill|Tatsumaki Jigoku|ja|別府地獄めぐり#龍巻地獄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsumaki Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5709027}}
